text:
Viewers were left stunned on Friday morning when one of the stars of the Today show announced they will be leaving Australia for the UK in August to chase "a dream of mine".

Today show newsreader and entertainment reporter Brooke Boney has announced she will be leaving the breakfast program in August to chase her dreams.

The 36-year-old journalist revealed to Channel Nine viewers on Friday just before 9am she had been offered a place to study at United Kingdom's Oxford University.

Boney was all smiles during the announcement, saying it was a "really difficult decision" but at the end of the day a lifelong dream she wanted to achieve.

"I don't want to go into too much right now, because there'll be plenty of time for goodbyes and thank yous," she said to hosts Karl Stefanovic and Sarah Abo.

"It's been a dream of mine to be able to study at an overseas university, and it just felt like the right time to take that step."

Boney joined the Nine breakfast team in 2019, after previously working as a political correspondent for the SBS, the broadcaster's NITV channel, ABC and Triple J.

She worked as the entertainment reporter for the network, after Richard Wilkins decided to take a step back and delegate some of his responsibilities to the bubbly star.

But in recent weeks Boney has taken a more central role by presenting the news on top of the local and international entertainment gossip.

Asked by Abo about how she felt landing a spot at one of the most prestigious universities in the world, Boney said she had "mixed emotions".

"I love coming in here. It's the most fun job in the world. We get to hang out with each other every day, but I'm also really excited to see what's out there in the world. Oh, my head is spinning right now. My heart's beating really fast," she said.

Boney - who is a proud Gamilaroi Gomeroi woman - said she never imagined working on one of Australia's most iconic shows in television.

"I've seen people get married, I've flown to London and rode a bus with Jason Momoa and Rome to talk to Dame Helen Mirren, I've covered the Oscars, I've done so much on this show," she said in a Nine statement.

"And to be able to sit there with Karl and Sarah and Alex (Cullen) every day, it's such a privilege and none of that is lost on me."

However, she has not ruled out returning to TV once she finishes her education.

The entertainment reporter revealed she is studying a Masters of Public Policy, which will take about two years, as it is "what fits best with my journalistic career thus far".

"I thought if I don't do this now, then I'm probably never going to. I am at that age where if I leave now and go and study and come back, I'll still only be 38," she said.

"That still leaves plenty of time to sort of carve out the next part of what my career is. I'd love to be able to come back and still do things with Nine."

The network's director of morning television, Steven Burling, in a statement said Nine are proud of what Boney has achieved in her time and are supportive of her move.

"A great broadcaster, a talented journalist and a passionate advocate for issues concerning Indigenous Australians and young women, Brooke will be missed by her co-hosts - who are also her great mates - and the entire production team," he said.

"We couldn't be prouder of Brooke as she embarks on this challenge in the latter part of the year, she inspires us all as she follows her dreams, wherever they take her."

Boney will leave the Today show after the Paris Olympics in August.

A replacement has not been named, but it is likely discussions were already underway in the executive team prior to the presenter's announcement on Friday.
